title: Built a Claude-powered quant research lab: 133 hypotheses, 68 backtests, 11 paper-traded strategies, 0 live promotions
author: Numerous_Pickle_9678
selftext:
Built this mostly with **Claude Code** as a quant research / portfolio-management workflow, but the goal was not “ask AI for trades and hope.”

The idea was to build a more disciplined research lab for systematic strategies:

* hypotheses get defined and tested
* weak ideas get rejected
* only robust ones make it to paper trading
* nothing goes live without surviving hard gates

Current state:

* **133 hypotheses** in scope
* **68 strategy variants** backtested
* **11 strategies** passed the robustness filter
* **11 are in paper trading**
* **0 have been promoted to live capital**

A few of the controls:

* Deflated Sharpe Ratio / multiple-testing penalty
* CPCV / OOS-vs-IS checks
* perturbation stability checks
* pre-trade risk limits
* append-only trade / experiment records
* daily reports committed to git

Main repo:

* [45ck/llm-quant](https://github.com/45ck/llm-quant)

It’s currently focused on liquid US ETFs + crypto, with Claude acting more like a structured researcher / PM / quant analyst inside a constrained pipeline rather than a magic black box.

Interested in criticism from people who’ve actually built or validated trading systems, especially around the research process, anti-overfitting controls, and what still looks weak.

comments.author: Numerous_Pickle_9678
comments.body:
Some actual stats from the current research funnel:

* **133 hypotheses** in scope
* **68 strategy variants** backtested over a 5-year window
* **11 passed all 5 robustness gates**
* **16% pass rate**
* **11 currently in paper trading**
* **0 promoted to live capital**

A few of the core gates:

* **Sharpe > 0.80** on Track A
* **MaxDD < 15%** on Track A
* **DSR >= 0.95**
* **CPCV OOS/IS > 0**
* **Perturbation stability >= 3/5**

Nothing goes live just because it looked good in backtests. All 11 passers are still in **paper trading** as of **2026-03-26**, and promotion requires **30+ days of paper track record** first.

# Passing Strategies (11 of 68 tested)

* **LQD-SPY credit lead** — Sharpe **1.250**, MaxDD **12.4%**, DSR **0.9950**, CPCV OOS/IS **1.023** — *IG bond → US equity*
* **AGG-SPY credit lead** — Sharpe **1.145**, MaxDD **8.4%**, DSR **0.9938**, CPCV OOS/IS **1.039** — *Total bond → US equity*
* **SPY overnight momentum** — Sharpe **1.043**, MaxDD **8.7%**, DSR **0.9506**, CPCV OOS/IS **1.011** — *Overnight gap microstructure*
* **AGG-QQQ credit lead** — Sharpe **1.080**, MaxDD **11.2%**, DSR **0.9894**, CPCV OOS/IS **1.031** — *Total bond → tech equity*
* **VCIT-QQQ credit lead** — Sharpe **1.037**, MaxDD **14.5%**, DSR **0.9820**, CPCV OOS/IS **1.010** — *Corp bond → tech equity*
* **LQD-QQQ credit lead** — Sharpe **1.023**, MaxDD **13.7%**, DSR **0.9824**, CPCV OOS/IS **1.031** — *IG bond → tech equity*
* **EMB-SPY credit lead** — Sharpe **1.005**, MaxDD **9.1%**, DSR **0.9802**, CPCV OOS/IS **0.980** — *EM sovereign → US equity*
* **HYG-SPY credit lead** — Sharpe **0.913**, MaxDD **14.7%**, DSR **0.9650**, CPCV OOS/IS **1.111** — *HY bond → US equity*
* **AGG-EFA credit lead** — Sharpe **0.860**, MaxDD **10.3%**, DSR **0.9656**, CPCV OOS/IS **1.134** — *Total bond → intl equity*
* **HYG-QQQ credit lead** — Sharpe **0.867**, MaxDD **13.4%**, DSR **0.9606**, CPCV OOS/IS **1.050** — *HY bond → tech equity*
* **SOXX-QQQ lead-lag** — Sharpe **0.861**, MaxDD **14.4%**, DSR **0.9603**, CPCV OOS/IS **0.819** — *Semis → tech equity*

One important caveat: **10 of the 11 passers share the same underlying credit-equity lead/lag mechanism**, so I’m not treating this as 11 independent edges.

comments.author: lukasTHEwise
comments.body: Very interesting project! It's been a month since you have posted this, did any of the 11 strategies pass the 30 day exam?

replies.author: Numerous_Pickle_9678
replies.body:
Honest update — no, none have passed yet.

As of last week's paper trading status report (2026-04-22):

* **0 of 51 strategies at the 30-day gate** (median elapsed: 7 days, median trades: 1)                                                                       - **0 at the 50-trade gate**
* Earliest started is `soxx-qqq-lead-lag` at **10 days / 1 trade** — still 20 trading days and 49 trades short
* **0 promoted to live capital**

Two things I found while checking, in the spirit of not hand-waving:

1. **The daily paper batch was running manually and had gaps.** No batch ran 2026-04-18 → 04-21; backfilled this week via a new `--date` flag. Fixing with a scheduled job so "days elapsed" stops being a lie.
2. **The 50-trade gate is the binding constraint, not the 30-day one.** At \~0.5 trades/strategy/week, 50 trades is a multi-month wait per strategy. Either I accept that, add higher-frequency mechanisms, or relax to "N trades OR M months, whichever later." Leaning toward documenting it honestly rather than moving the goalposts.

So the headline from the original post — **0 promoted** — still stands, and will for a while. That's the funnel working as designed, not a failure. Backtest + robustness = *promising*. Paper + live = *proven*. Nothing here is proven yet.

Will post another update when the first strategy actually clears the gate (or gets killed trying).
